## NMState Configuration Examples

### VLAN

```yaml=
nodes:
  - hostname: sno
    rootDeviceHints:
      deviceName: /dev/nvme0n1
    interfaces:
      - name: enp97s0f0
        mac_address: D0:50:99:DD:58:95
    networkConfig:
      interfaces:
        - name: enp97s0f0.70
          type: vlan
          state: up
          vlan:
            id: 70
            base-iface: enp97s0f0
          ipv4:
            enabled: true
            address:
              - ip: 192.168.70.46
                prefix-length: 23
            dhcp: false
        - name: enp97s0f0
          type: ethernet
          state: up
          mac-address: D0:50:99:DD:58:95
      routes:
        config:
          - destination: 0.0.0.0/0
            next-hop-address: 192.168.70.1
            next-hop-interface: enp97s0f0.70
            table-id: 254
```

### Bond

```yaml=
nodes:
  - hostname: sno
    rootDeviceHints:
      deviceName: /dev/nvme0n1
    interfaces:
      - name: enp97s0f0
        mac_address: D0:50:99:DD:58:95
      - name: enp97s0f1
        mac_address: D0:50:99:DD:58:96
    networkConfig:
      interfaces:
        - name: bond0
          type: bond
          state: up
          ipv4:
            address:
              - ip: 192.168.70.46
                prefix-length: 23
            dhcp: false
            enabled: true
          link-aggregation:
            # mode=1 active-backup
            # mode=2 balance-xor
            # mode=4 802.3ad
            # mode=5 balance-tlb
            # mode=6 balance-alb
            mode: 802.3ad
            port:
              - enp97s0f0
              - enp97s0f1

        - name: enp97s0f0
          type: ethernet
          state: up
          mac-address: D0:50:99:DD:58:95
        - name: enp97s0f1
          type: ethernet
          state: up
          mac-address: D0:50:99:DD:58:96
      routes:
        config:
          - destination: 0.0.0.0/0
            next-hop-address: 192.168.70.1
            next-hop-interface: bond0
            table-id: 254
```

### Bond + VLAN

```yaml=
nodes:
  - hostname: sno
    rootDeviceHints:
      deviceName: /dev/nvme0n1
    interfaces:
      - name: enp97s0f0
        mac_address: D0:50:99:DD:58:95
      - name: enp97s0f1
        mac_address: D0:50:99:DD:58:96
    networkConfig:
      interfaces:
        - name: bond0.70
          type: vlan
          state: up
          vlan:
            id: 70
            base-iface: bond0
          ipv4:
            enabled: true
            address:
              - ip: 192.168.70.46
                prefix-length: 23
            dhcp: false

        - name: bond0
          type: bond
          state: up
          link-aggregation:
            # mode=1 active-backup
            # mode=2 balance-xor
            # mode=4 802.3ad
            # mode=5 balance-tlb
            # mode=6 balance-alb
            mode: 802.3ad
            port:
              - enp97s0f0
              - enp97s0f1

        - name: enp97s0f0
          type: ethernet
          state: up
          mac-address: D0:50:99:DD:58:95
        - name: enp97s0f1
          type: ethernet
          state: up
          mac-address: D0:50:99:DD:58:96
      routes:
        config:
          - destination: 0.0.0.0/0
            next-hop-address: 192.168.70.1
            next-hop-interface: bond0.70
            table-id: 254
```
",
        "sections": [
          {
            "title": "OpenShift Agent Based Installer Helper",
            "content": "
This repo holds some utilities to easily leverage the OpenShift Agent-Based Installer.  Supports bare metal, vSphere, and platform=none deployments in SNO/3 Node/HA configurations.

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "Prerequisites",
            "content": "
- A RHEL system to work from
- OpenShift CLI Tools - run `./download-openshift-cli.sh` then `sudo cp ./bin/* /usr/local/bin/`
- NMState CLI `dnf install nmstate`
- Ansible Core `dnf install ansible-core` - or AAP
- Ansible Collections for the automation: `ansible-galaxy install -r execution-environment/collections/requirements.yml`
- Red Hat OpenShift Pull Secret saved to a file: https://console.redhat.com/openshift/downloads#tool-pull-secret
- Any other Pull Secret for a disconnected registry, joined with the Red Hat OpenShift Pull Secret.  [Handy script to join pull secret files](https://github.com/kenmoini/disconnected-openshift/blob/main/scripts/join-auths.sh).

",
            "level": 2
          },
          {
            "title": "Alternative: Pre-built Execution Environment",
            "content": "
For offline/portable execution or use with Ansible Automation Platform, use the pre-built containerized Ansible Execution Environment:

```bash
",
            "level": 3
          },
          {
            "title": "Pull latest version",
            "content": "podman pull quay.io/takinosh/openshift-agent-install-ee:latest

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "Or pull a specific release version",
            "content": "podman pull quay.io/takinosh/openshift-agent-install-ee:v4.21.0

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "Run playbook in container",
            "content": "podman run --rm -it \
  -v $(pwd):/runner \
  -v ~/pull-secret.json:/runner/pull-secret.json:ro \
  quay.io/takinosh/openshift-agent-install-ee:latest \
  ansible-playbook -e @examples/sno-4.20-standard/cluster.yml \
                   -e @examples/sno-4.20-standard/nodes.yml \
                   playbooks/create-manifests.yml
```

**EE Image Versions:**
- `latest` - Always points to the most recent release
- `vX.Y.Z` - Pinned semantic version tags (e.g., v4.21.0, v4.22.0)
- Automatically built on release tag pushes

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "Supported OpenShift Versions",
            "content": "
This tooling supports OpenShift 4.15 and newer. The `download-openshift-cli.sh` script automatically downloads the latest stable version of the OpenShift CLI tools.

Tested and validated with:
- OpenShift 4.20.x
- OpenShift 4.21.x

Examples are provided for different deployment patterns:
- `examples/serenity-sno.v60.lab.kemo.network/` - SNO with disconnected registry
- `examples/sno-4.20-standard/` - Standard SNO 4.20 deployment
- `examples/ha-4.21-disconnected/` - HA 4.21 disconnected deployment

",
            "level": 2
          },
          {
            "title": "Version Validation and Compatibility Testing",
            "content": "
This repository includes automated tools to validate manifest generation across multiple OpenShift versions and detect version-specific API changes.

",
            "level": 2
          },
          {
            "title": "Quick Start",
            "content": "
```bash
",
            "level": 3
          },
          {
            "title": "Generate manifests for multiple OpenShift versions",
            "content": "./hack/generate-version-manifests.sh sno-disconnected "4.19 4.20 4.21"

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "Validate manifests against deployment standards",
            "content": "./hack/validate-deployment-standards.sh \
  ~/generated_assets/version-compare/sno-disconnected-4.20 4.20

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "Compare critical version boundaries",
            "content": "./hack/compare-version-manifests.sh 4.19 4.20 sno-disconnected
./hack/compare-version-manifests.sh 4.20 4.21 sno-disconnected
```

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "Critical Version Boundaries",
            "content": "
**4.19 → 4.20**: ImageDigestMirrorSet migration
- Disconnected deployments must migrate from `imageDigestSources` in install-config.yaml to standalone `ImageDigestMirrorSet` manifest

**4.20 → 4.21**: OpenShiftSDN removal
- All deployments must use `networkType: OVNKubernetes` (OpenShiftSDN removed completely)

",
            "level": 3
          },
          {
            "title": "GitHub Actions Integration",
            "content": "
The version validation workflow automatically runs on PRs that modify templates or examples:

```bash
",
            "level": 3
          },
          {
            "title": "Manually trigger validation with GitHub issue creation",
            "content": "gh workflow run version-validation.yml \
  -f create_issues=true \
  -f examples="sno-disconnected ha-4.21-disconnected sno-4.20-standard"
```

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "LLM-Powered Validation",
            "content": "
Version validation uses **Granite-3-2-8b-instruct** LLM to provide intelligent analysis of:
- API compliance per OpenShift version
- Deployment pattern standards (SNO, 3-Node, HA)
- Connectivity requirements (connected, disconnected, proxy)
- Platform-specific configuration validation

Example validation output:
```
[PASS] Image Registry Configuration
[FAIL] Network Configuration - networkType: OpenShiftSDN deprecated for 4.21
[PASS] Platform Configuration
[PASS] Deployment Topology
```

",
            "level": 3
          },
          {
            "title": "Documentation",
            "content": "
- **[Version Compatibility Matrix](docs/version-compatibility-matrix.md)** - API changes, migration paths, feature support
- **[Version Validation Feature](docs/version-validation-feature.md)** - Complete feature documentation
- **[Quick Start Guide](docs/version-validation-quick-start.md)** - Step-by-step usage instructions
- **[Cheat Sheet](VERSION_VALIDATION_CHEATSHEET.md)** - Quick reference commands

",
            "level": 3
          },
          {
            "title": "DNS Setup",
            "content": "
This project uses **dnsmasq** as a lightweight DNS server for OpenShift cluster deployments. For each cluster, only 3 DNS records are needed:
- `api.<cluster_name>.<domain>` → API VIP
- `api-int.<cluster_name>.<domain>` → API VIP
- `*.apps.<cluster_name>.<domain>` → App VIP

",
            "level": 2
          },
          {
            "title": "Quick DNS Setup",
            "content": "
```bash
",
            "level": 3
          },
          {
            "title": "Install and configure dnsmasq",
            "content": "sudo ./hack/setup-dnsmasq.sh

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "Add DNS entries for your cluster",
            "content": "sudo ./hack/configure-dnsmasq-entries.sh add examples/sno-4.20-standard/cluster.yml

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "Test DNS resolution",
            "content": "dig @localhost api.sno-4-20.example.com
```

For detailed DNS configuration, troubleshooting, and migration from FreeIPA, see [DNS Setup Guide](docs/dns-setup.md).

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "E2E Testing and Bootstrap",
            "content": "
For end-to-end testing in a KVM environment with automated infrastructure setup:

```bash
",
            "level": 2
          },
          {
            "title": "Bootstrap complete environment (installs packages, DNS, VyOS router, etc.)",
            "content": "sudo ./e2e-tests/bootstrap_env.sh

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "Run E2E tests",
            "content": "./e2e-tests/run_e2e.sh
```

The bootstrap script automatically sets up dnsmasq for DNS resolution, replacing the previous FreeIPA-based approach.

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "Usage - Declarative",
            "content": "
In the `examples` directory you'll find sample cluster configuration variables.  By defining the cluster in its own folder with the `cluster.yml` and `nodes.yml` files, you can easily template and generate the ABI ISO in one shot with:

```bash
",
            "level": 2
          },
          {
            "title": "Optionally, change the path to the site configs from the default ./examples",
            "content": "export SITE_CONFIG_DIR="./site-configs"

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "Create an ISO from the defined cluster.yml and nodes.yml file",
            "content": "./hack/create-iso.sh $FOLDER_NAME

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "Available examples:",
            "content": "",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "- serenity-sno.v60.lab.kemo.network  (SNO with disconnected registry)",
            "content": "",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "- sno-4.20-standard                   (Standard SNO 4.20 connected deployment)",
            "content": "",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "- ha-4.21-disconnected                (HA 4.21 disconnected deployment)",
            "content": "```

This script will take those defined files, generate the templates with Ansible, create the ISO, and present next step instructions.

Alternatively, you can perform those steps manually with the instructions below.

---

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "Usage - Manual",
            "content": "
",
            "level": 2
          },
          {
            "title": "1. Templating Agent Based Installer Manifests",
            "content": "
You can quickly and easily template the ABI manifests with the provided `create-manifests.yml` Ansible Playbook.

```bash=
",
            "level": 3
          },
          {
            "title": "Make sure you're in the `playbooks` directory",
            "content": "cd playbooks/

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "Execute the automation with your custom cluster configuration set in a YAML file",
            "content": "ansible-playbook -e "@your-cluster-vars.yml" create-manifests.yml
```

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "2. Creating the Agent Installer ISO",
            "content": "
After running the automation to generate the manifests, you can create the ISO with the following:

```bash=
",
            "level": 3
          },
          {
            "title": "Create the ISO",
            "content": "openshift-install agent create image --dir ./generated_manifests/<cluster_name>

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "Watch the Bootstrap process",
            "content": "openshift-install agent wait-for bootstrap-complete --dir ./generated_manifests/<cluster_name>

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "Watch the installation process",
            "content": "openshift-install agent wait-for install-complete --dir ./generated_manifests/<cluster_name>
```

You'll need to provide it some variables such as the following:

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "General Configuration Variables",
            "content": "
```yaml=
",
            "level": 4
          },
          {
            "title": "pull_secret path is the path to the pull-secret for the cluster",
            "content": "pull_secret_path: ~/ocp-install-pull-secret.json

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "ssh_public_key_path is the path to the SSH public key to use for the cluster",
            "content": "",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "if this is not set then a new key pair will be generated",
            "content": "",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "ssh_public_key_path: ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub",
            "content": "
",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "Cluster metadata",
            "content": "base_domain: d70.kemo.labs
cluster_name: suki-sno

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "platform_type is the type of platform to use for the cluster (baremetal, vsphere, none)",
            "content": "",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "must be none for SNO",
            "content": "platform_type: none

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "VIPs - set as a list in case this is a dual-stack cluster",
            "content": "api_vips:
  - 192.168.70.46

app_vips:
  - 192.168.70.46

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "Optional NTP Servers",
            "content": "ntp_servers:
  - deep-thought.kemo.labs

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "Optional DNS Server definitions",
            "content": "dns_servers:
  - 192.168.42.9
  - 192.168.42.10
dns_search_domains:
  - kemo.labs
  - kemo.network

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "cluster_network_cidr is the overall CIDR space for the Pods in the cluster",
            "content": "cluster_network_cidr: 10.128.0.0/14
",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "cluster_network_host_prefix is the number of bits in the cluster_network_cidr that are for each node",
            "content": "cluster_network_host_prefix: 23

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "service_network_cidrs is the CIDR space for the Services in the cluster (ClusterIP/NodePort/LoadBalancer)",
            "content": "service_network_cidrs:
  - 172.30.0.0/16

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "machine_network_cidr is the CIDR space for the Machines in the cluster",
            "content": "machine_network_cidrs:
  - 192.168.70.0/23

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "network_type is the network provider for the cluster",
            "content": "",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "OpenShift 4.21+: OVNKubernetes (REQUIRED - OpenShiftSDN removed)",
            "content": "",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "OpenShift 4.15-4.20: OVNKubernetes (recommended) or OpenShiftSDN (deprecated)",
            "content": "network_type: OVNKubernetes

",
            "level": 1
          },
          {
            "title": "rendezvous_ip is the IP address of the node that will be used for the bootstrap node",
            "content": "rendezvous_ip: 192.168.70.46

    source: registry.connect.redhat.com

",

",
            "level": 3
          },
          {
            "title": "Bond",
            "content": "
```yaml=
nodes:
  - hostname: sno
    rootDeviceHints:
      deviceName: /dev/nvme0n1
    interfaces:
      - name: enp97s0f0
        mac_address: D0:50:99:DD:58:95
      - name: enp97s0f1
        mac_address: D0:50:99:DD:58:96
    networkConfig:
      interfaces:
        - name: bond0
          type: bond
          state: up
          ipv4:
            address:
              - ip: 192.168.70.46
                prefix-length: 23
            dhcp: false
            enabled: true
          link-aggregation:
            # mode=1 active-backup
            # mode=2 balance-xor
            # mode=4 802.3ad
            # mode=5 balance-tlb
            # mode=6 balance-alb
            mode: 802.3ad
            port:
              - enp97s0f0
              - enp97s0f1

        - name: enp97s0f0
          type: ethernet
          state: up
          mac-address: D0:50:99:DD:58:95
        - name: enp97s0f1
          type: ethernet
          state: up
          mac-address: D0:50:99:DD:58:96
      routes:
        config:
          - destination: 0.0.0.0/0
            next-hop-address: 192.168.70.1
            next-hop-interface: bond0
            table-id: 254
```
